source: Representative DVWA walkthrough constructed from the standard DVWA exercises (auth -> SQLi -> file upload webshell -> command exec). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 / engagement dry-run; NOT a live capture. DVWA is the recommended FIRST live target (single web-app container).
vulnhub_correspondence: DVWA login (admin/password) -> SQLi on /vulnerabilities/sqli dumps the user hashes -> file-upload page drops a PHP webshell -> command execution as www-data -> read /etc/passwd.

steps.actor_intent: Scan the target with nmap to find the web service.
steps.tool_output:
PORT   STATE SERVICE VERSION
80/tcp open  http    Apache httpd 2.4.25 ((Debian))
3306/tcp open mysql MySQL 5.5
http://dvwa.lab/ [200 OK] Apache[2.4.25], PHP[7.0], Title[Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)]

steps.actor_intent: Log in to DVWA with the default admin credentials.
steps.tool_output:
HTTP/1.1 302 Found
Set-Cookie: PHPSESSID=abc; path=/
Location: /index.php

steps.actor_intent: Enumerate the vulnerability modules to find the SQLi and upload pages.
Inspect the SQLi page to find the id parameter.
steps.tool_output: <form action='#' method='GET'><input type='text' name='id'><input type='submit' name='Submit' value='Submit'></form>

steps.actor_intent: Exploit the SQL injection in the id parameter to dump the user hashes.
steps.tool_output:
parameter 'id' is injectable
the back-end DBMS is MySQL and is vulnerable
Database: dvwa
Table: users
| admin | 5f4dcc3b5aa765d61d8327deb882cf99 |
| gordonb | e99a18c428cb38d5f260853678922e03 |

steps.actor_intent: Upload a PHP webshell through the file upload page.
Run id through the uploaded webshell to confirm execution.
candidate_pool: Run id through the uploaded webshell to confirm execution.
Log in to DVWA again with the default credentials.
Fingerprint the web application technology again.
Read the root flag with no root access.

steps.actor_intent: Read /etc/passwd through the webshell to enumerate users.
